recalls.Description: IKEA is recalling its 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic presses due to potential safety hazards. These garlic presses feature a black rubber handle and a zinc-coated chamber, with the IKEA logo located on the upper part of the handle. Only the models displaying this logo are included in the recall.
recalls.Title: IKEA Recalls VÄRDEFULL Garlic Presses Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach IKEA at 888-966-4532 from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls. Alternatively, go to www.ikea.com/us/en and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL Garlic Press
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 43,830 (in addition, about 10,700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic press
Recalled IKEA 365+ VÄRDEFULL garlic press logo identification

Injuries.Name: IKEA has received a total of 10 incident reports globally, including three reports of lacerations and finger splinters. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at IKEA stores across the nation and online at IKEA.com from March 2025 to May 2025 for approximately $8.

Distributors.Name: IKEA US Retail LLC, of Woodbridge, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or ingestion from detached metal pieces

Remedies.Name: Stop using the garlic press and return it to any IKEA store for a full refund. If returning is not possible, contact IKEA for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. No proof of purchase is needed.

recalls.metaDescription: IKEA recalls VÄRDEFULL garlic presses due to laceration and ingestion hazards. Return for a full refund. Learn more here.

recalls.Description: Greater Goods is recalling its Digital Kitchen Scales due to safety concerns related to coin batteries. The affected models include 0480, 0455, 0456, 0458, 0473, 0479, 0481, 0747, 0748, 0749, 0751, 0752, and 0754. These scales are available in various colors such as gray, black, blue, green, pink, red, silver, and white, and feature the Greater Goods logo on the front panel. Each scale comes with a pre-installed lithium CR3032 coin battery and an additional spare battery in the packaging.
recalls.Title: Greater Goods Recalls Digital Kitchen Scales for Ingestion Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Greater Goods at 866-991-0397 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, email recall@greatergoods.com, or visit www.greatergoods.com and click the recall banner or go to https://shop.greatergoods.com/pages/replaceyourbatterydoor for further details.

Recalled Black Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Cherry Red Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Black Glass Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Silver Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Blue Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Mint Green Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Pink Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled White Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Green Digital Kitchen Scale
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale Packaging – Back (Sticker color will match Scale color)
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale Packaging – Front (Sticker color will match Scale color
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale without battery door - Bottom
Recalled Digital Kitchen Scale - Bottom
Recalled battery door and updated battery door

Retailers.Name: Available at Alexandra's Kitchen in Niskayuna, NY, and online at Amazon, Walmart, and Greater Goods from May 2023 through September 2024 for approximately $10.

Importers.Name: Greater Goods LLC, of Saint Louis, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible lithium coin batteries and non-child resistant packaging, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should remove the battery from the scale and keep it out of children's reach. Contact Greater Goods for a free recall kit, including a secure battery door, necessary labeling, and an updated manual.

recalls.metaDescription: Greater Goods recalls Digital Kitchen Scales due to ingestion hazards from accessible coin batteries. Learn how to get a free recall kit.

recalls.Description: Walmart has announced a recall of the Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers, identified by model number MS14100094536S1. These rechargeable, cordless choppers feature a green lid, a white body, and a clear plastic bowl. Each unit includes a blade attachment with three curved blades mounted on a white plastic spindle. The model number can be found on the label located on the bottom of the clear bowl.
recalls.Title: Walmart Issues Recall for Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily, or visit their website at https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper container with blade and lid
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled
Recalled Mainstays Electric Mini Chopper assembled

Injuries.Name: Walmart has received five reports of lacerations, two of which required medical attention, including stitches. Injuries occurred during assembly of the product or during cleaning/handling of the product.

Manufacturers.Name: Shuangma Plastic Manufacturing Inc., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations across the country and online at Walmart.com from August 2022 through October 2023, priced between $10 and $15.

Importers.Name: Walmart Stores Inc., of Bentonville, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Unexpected blade operation during assembly or when not enclosed, leading to a laceration risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled choppers immediately and contact Walmart for a full refund. Choppers can be returned to any Walmart store for a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Walmart recalls Mainstays Electric Mini Choppers due to laceration hazards. Stop using and contact Walmart for a refund.
